source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crown Motion Compensator

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Walker Ridge Block 634

event.top_event: Structural damage to Crown Motion Compensator with falling object hazard
actions_taken: Drill floor designated as No Entry Zone
Derrick inspected for broken or loose items
CMC procedure reviewed and updated by Transocean
NOV software logic updated and installed
Function test performed to verify CMC valve after software upgrade
event.summary: On September 21, 2020, at approximately 2100 hours, the Driller on the Transocean Deepwater Conqueror attempted to take drill string weight with the Crown Motion Compensator (CMC) while the CMC cylinders were at mid-stroke. The Driller opened the CMC valve without fully retracting the cylinders to the beams as required by procedure. The CMC rapidly dropped 13 feet, shearing all 7 bolts on the starboard equalizing bumper assembly bracket (48 pounds), which fell 203 feet to the drill floor. The port bumper assembly had 6 of 7 bolts sheared. A software logic error in the CMC auto-equalizing feature prevented pressure equalization before the Olmsted valve opened, causing the rapid retraction. No injuries were reported.
recommendations: Transocean updated CMC-related procedures to ensure correct setup and sequences
Implemented rig-specific CMC training for Toolpushers, Drillers, Assistant Drillers, and Derrickmen
NOV updated software logic to prevent unintentional CMC valve opening if differential pressure exists

hazards.name: Pressurized hydraulic and pneumatic systems in Crown Motion Compensator
Elevated equipment and structures in derrick

threats.name: Pressure differential between air and hydraulic systems in CMC
CMC valve opened while cylinders at mid-stroke
Software logic error in CMC auto-equalizing feature
Driller did not follow slip and cut procedure

consequences.name: Rapid uncontrolled descent of CMC assembly
Shearing of equalizing bumper assembly bolts
Falling object (48-pound bumper assembly) from 203 feet
Operational shutdown and loss of production

controls.name: Slip and Cut Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring CMC cylinders to be fully retracted to beams before proceeding
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement to prevent pressure differential buildup

supporting_text: According to the slip and cut procedure, the Driller is responsible for lowering the CMC cylinders back to the beams before proceeding forward to the next step.
The Driller did not follow Transocean's Slip & Cut procedure by retracting the CMC cylinders all the way down to the beams before proceding forward to the next step.

controls.name: CMC Auto-Equalizing Safety System
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to automatically equalize pressure differential before valve opening
controls.lod_basis: Built-in safety system designed to prevent excessive pressure differential

supporting_text: The CMC has a built-in safety system to automatically equalize pressures if there was an imbalance of more than 30 psi exists, when the CMC is commanded to open.
National Oilwell Varco (NOV) confirmed there was a software logic error which prevented the pressure from equalizing prior to opening the CMC / Olmsted valve.
The CMC auto equalizing feature did not work as designed, allowing the Olmsted Valve to only open 44 percent for approximately 2 seconds then quickly closing again prior to the system equalizing.

controls.name: Pre-Job Safety Meeting and Job Safety Analysis
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to brief crew on hazards and procedures
controls.lod_basis: Safety communication and awareness prior to high-risk operation

supporting_text: From approximately 1800 hours to 2000 hours, a safety meeting was held prior to the slip and cut operation and a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was reviewed and signed by all participating rig crew members involved.
There was a pre job safety meeting held with the 'D' drill crew prior to the slipping and cutting 155 foot of 2 1/8" drill line.

controls.name: Drawworks Brake Test and Calibration
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to verify equipment functionality
controls.lod_basis: Procedural verification of critical lifting equipment

supporting_text: the drawworks was calibrated with a brake test performed as per procedure

controls.name: Derrick Inspection for Loose Items
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to detect and remove hazards after incident
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident inspection to prevent secondary incidents

supporting_text: All areas on and around the CMC and derrick structure were being inspected for broken or loose items.
the entire drill floor being designated as a 'No Entry Zone' while the derrick was being inspected

controls.name: Updated CMC Software Logic
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent unintentional valve opening with pressure differential
controls.lod_basis: Software fix to address root cause of auto-equalizing failure

supporting_text: NOV has updated and installed the new software logic in relation to the safety interlocks of the Olmsted valve opening when it is activated.
The software upgrade will prevent the CMC valve from unintentionally opening, if there is a differential pressure over the CMC valve in any direction.
A successful function test was performed to verify and test the CMC valve after the software upgrade.

controls.name: Enhanced CMC Training Program
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to improve operator competence and procedure compliance
controls.lod_basis: Training to prevent human error in CMC operation

supporting_text: Transocean has also implemented rig specific CMC on the job training with additional training for the Toolpushers, Drillers, Assistant Drillers and Derrickmen.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
